info.title: Local Explorer API
info.description: Local subset of Cloudflare API for exploring resources during local development.

get.description: Returns the namespaces owned by an account.

schema.description: Limits the number of keys returned in the response. The cursor attribute may be used to iterate over the next batch of keys if there are more than the limit.
Filters returned keys by a name prefix. Exact matches and any key names that begin with the prefix will be returned.

Opaque token indicating the position from which to continue when requesting the next set of records if the amount of list results was limited by the limit parameter. A valid value for the cursor can be obtained from the `cursors` object in the `result_info` structure.

4XX.description: List a Namespace's Keys response failure.

get.description: Returns the value associated with the given key in the given namespace. Use URL-encoding to use special characters (for example, `:`, `!`, `%`) in the key name. If the KV-pair is set to expire at some point, the expiration time as measured in seconds since the UNIX epoch will be returned in the `expiration` response header.

put.description: Write a value identified by a key. Use URL-encoding to use special characters (for example, `:`, `!`, `%`) in the key name. Body should be the value to be stored. If JSON metadata to be associated with the key/value pair is needed, use `multipart/form-data` content type for your PUT request (see dropdown below in `REQUEST BODY SCHEMA`). Existing values, expirations, and metadata will be overwritten. If neither `expiration` nor `expiration_ttl` is specified, the key-value pair will never expire. If both are set, `expiration_ttl` is used and `expiration` is ignored.

200.description: Write key-value pair with metadata response.

4XX.description: Write key-value pair with metadata response failure.

put.summary: Write key-value pair with optional metadata

delete.description: Remove a KV pair from the namespace. Use URL-encoding to use special characters (for example, `:`, `!`, `%`) in the key name.

post.description: Retrieve up to 100 KV pairs from the namespace. Keys must contain text-based values. JSON values can optionally be parsed instead of being returned as a string value. Metadata can be included if `withMetadata` is true.

keys.description: Array of keys to retrieve (maximum of 100).

4XX.description: Get multiple key-value pairs response failure.

get.description: Returns a list of D1 databases.

schema.description: a database name to search for.

count.description: Total number of results for the requested service

post.description: Returns the query result rows as arrays rather than objects. This is a performance-optimized version of the /query endpoint.

get.description: Returns the Durable Object namespaces owned by an account.
Returns the Durable Objects in a given namespace.

schema.description: The number of objects to return. The cursor attribute may be used to iterate over the next batch of objects if there are more than the limit.
Opaque token indicating the position from which to continue when requesting the next set of records. A valid value for the cursor can be obtained from the cursors object in the result_info structure.

count.description: Total results returned based on your list parameters.
Total results returned based on your list parameters.

get.description: Lists all R2 buckets on your account.
Gets properties of an existing R2 bucket.
List objects in an R2 bucket with optional prefix and delimiter.

parameters.name: per_page
parameters.description: Maximum number of objects to return

delete.description: Delete multiple objects from an R2 bucket.

schema.description: Array of object keys to delete

delete.summary: Delete Multiple Objects from R2 Bucket

get.description: Get an object from an R2 bucket. Use cf-metadata-only header for HEAD-like behavior.

parameters.name: cf-metadata-only
parameters.description: Set to 'true' to return only metadata (HEAD-like behavior)

put.description: Upload an object to an R2 bucket.

post.description: Execute SQL queries against a Durable Object's SQLite storage.

get.description: List all workers in the local dev registry.
Returns the workflows configured for local development.
Returns details of a specific workflow including instance status counts.

delete.description: Deletes all instances of a workflow by removing their persistence files.

get.description: Returns the instances of a workflow.

id.description: Optional instance ID. If not provided, a UUID is generated.

params.description: Optional JSON payload to pass to the workflow.

id.description: The instance ID of the newly created workflow instance.

get.description: Returns the status details of a workflow instance.

4XX.description: Get Workflow Instance Details response failure.

delete.description: Deletes a workflow instance by removing its persistence files.

patch.description: Changes the status of a workflow instance (pause, resume, restart, terminate).

action.description: The action to perform on the workflow instance.

4XX.description: Change Workflow Instance Status response failure.

post.description: Sends an event to a workflow instance.

schema.description: Optional JSON payload for the event.

success.description: Whether the API call was successful.
Whether the API call was successful.

workers_cursor.description: Opaque token indicating the position from which to continue when requesting the next set of records. A valid value for the cursor can be obtained from the cursors object in the result_info structure.

hasStoredData.description: Whether the Durable Object has stored data.

success.description: Whether the API call was successful.

count.description: Total number of results for the requested service.

page.description: Current page within paginated list of results.

per_page.description: Number of results per page of results.

total_count.description: Total results available without any search parameters.

total_pages.description: The number of total pages in the entire result set.

success.description: Whether the API call was successful.

changed_db.description: Denotes if the database has been altered in some way, like deleting rows.

changes.description: Rough indication of how many rows were modified by the query, as provided by SQLite's `sqlite3_total_changes()`.

duration.description: The duration of the SQL query execution inside the database. Does not include any network communication.

last_row_id.description: The row ID of the last inserted row in a table with an `INTEGER PRIMARY KEY` as provided by SQLite. Tables created with `WITHOUT ROWID` do not populate this.

rows_read.description: Number of rows read during the SQL query execution, including indices (not all rows are necessarily returned).

rows_written.description: Number of rows written during the SQL query execution, including indices.

size_after.description: Size of the database after the query committed, in bytes.

sql_duration_ms.description: The duration of the SQL query execution inside the database. Does not include any network communication.

d1_batch-query.description: A single query object or a batch query object

d1_single-query.description: A single query with or without parameters
d1_single-query.title: single query

d1_sql.description: Your SQL query. Supports multiple statements, joined by semicolons, which will be executed as a batch.
d1_sql.example: SELECT * FROM myTable WHERE field = ? OR field = ?;

success.description: Whether the API call was successful

d1_jurisdiction-nullable.description: Specify the location to restrict the D1 database to run and store data. If this option is present, the location hint is ignored.

success.description: Whether the API call was successful

allOf.description: The metadata associated with the key.
The value associated with the key.

values.description: Requested keys are paired with their values and metadata in an object.

workers-kv_expiration.description: Expires the key at a certain time, measured in number of seconds since the UNIX epoch.

additionalProperties.description: The value associated with the key.

values.description: Requested keys are paired with their values in an object.

workers-kv_key_name_bulk.description: A key's name. The name may be at most 512 bytes. All printable, non-whitespace characters are valid.

success.description: Whether the API call was successful.

allOf.description: Associates arbitrary JSON data with a key/value pair.

workers-kv_value.description: A byte sequence to be stored, up to 25 MiB in length.

workers-kv_key_name.description: A key's name. The name may be at most 512 bytes. All printable, non-whitespace characters are valid. Use percent-encoding to define key names as part of a URL.

count.description: Total results returned based on your list parameters.

workers-kv_cursor.description: Opaque token indicating the position from which to continue when requesting the next set of records if the amount of list results was limited by the limit parameter. A valid value for the cursor can be obtained from the cursors object in the result_info structure.

workers-kv_key.description: A name for a value. A value stored under a given key may be retrieved via the same key.

expiration.description: The time, measured in number of seconds since the UNIX epoch, at which the key will expire. This property is omitted for keys that will not expire.

allOf.description: Arbitrary JSON that is associated with a key.

success.description: Whether the API call was successful.

workers-kv_namespace_title.description: A human-readable string name for a Namespace.

count.description: Total number of results for the requested service.

delimited.description: Common prefixes when using delimiter (virtual directories)

cursor.description: Cursor for fetching next page of results

is_truncated.description: Whether there are more results to fetch

params.description: Optional parameters for the SQL query

durable_object_id.description: Hex string ID of the Durable Object

queries.description: Array of SQL queries to execute

durable_object_name.description: Name to derive DO ID via idFromName()

queries.description: Array of SQL queries to execute

columns.description: Column names from the query result

rows.description: Array of row arrays containing query results

rows_read.description: Number of rows read during query execution

rows_written.description: Number of rows written during query execution

host.description: Hostname the worker is running on

isSelf.description: Whether this worker is the one hosting the explorer

name.description: Worker name from the dev registry

port.description: Port the worker is running on

workflows_instance-id.description: The unique identifier of a workflow instance.

class_name.description: The entrypoint class name of the workflow.

script_name.description: The script name containing the workflow.

id.description: The unique identifier of the workflow instance.

status.description: The current status of the instance.

created_on.description: ISO 8601 timestamp of when the instance was created.

id.description: The unique identifier of the workflow instance.

status.description: The current status of the instance.

output.description: Output value if the workflow completed successfully.

error.description: Error details if the workflow errored.
